title: Iliad, Book 3: The Duel of Paris and Menelaus
description: Book 3 of Homer's Iliad: The Duel of Paris and Menelaus.
introduction: Book 3 of the Iliad: The Duel of Paris and Menelaus. The Greek text is from the Venetus A manuscript (10th century), the oldest complete manuscript of the Iliad.

translation.original_text: Αὐτὰρ ἐπεὶ κόσμηθεν ἅμ᾿ ἡγεμόνεσσιν ἕκαστοι, Τρῶες μὲν κλαγγῇ τ᾿ ἐνοπῇ τ᾿ ἴσαν.
translation.text: When the armies were marshaled under their leaders, the Trojans came on with clamor and with a cry like birds. Paris stepped out before the Trojan lines to challenge any Greek to single combat. Menelaus saw him and rejoiced, like a lion finding a great carcass.
